title: Anna, Thy Charms.
content:
Tune - "Bonnie Mary."



        Anna, thy charms my bosom fire,
            And waste my soul with care;
        But ah! how bootless to admire,
            When fated to despair!
        Yet in thy presence, lovely fair,
            To hope may be forgiv'n;
        For sure 'twere impious to despair,
            So much in sight of Heav'n.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Anna, Thy Charms.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
